headline: xAI/Grok FLOOR CONFIRMATION with Musk distillation-admission INT notation. Composite remains 0. NEW PRIMARY-SOURCE EVIDENCE since May 3: Elon Musk admitted UNDER OATH on April 30 2026 at the Musk v. Altman trial that xAI used OpenAI model distillation to train Grok ('Partly,' in response to direct yes/no question). This contradicts xAI's prior public framing of independent model development. Per floor protocol, INT subdim already at 1 — admission cannot move composite below floor — but the trial-record under-oath admission is the first directly-attributable primary-source statement on the question and is appended to the floor-exit-criteria record. CSAM/California investigation evidence sustained. Floor confirmed.
floor_protocol_note: Floor-designated entity. The Musk under-oath admission (April 30, Musk v. Altman trial) of OpenAI model distillation is a new primary-source INT-dimension event since May 3 assessment. INT already at 1 — admission cannot move composite below floor. Notation: this is qualitatively distinct from prior CSAM and noncompliance evidence; it is a documented internal-knowledge / public-statement contradiction (I1 anchor 1, I2 anchor 1) at the highest evidence tier (sworn trial testimony).
key_evidence: April 30 2026 Musk v. Altman trial testimony: when asked under oath whether xAI used OpenAI model distillation to train Grok, Musk answered 'Partly.' First on-record admission contradicting xAI's prior public framing.
MIT Technology Review Week 1 trial summary: Musk admits xAI distills OpenAI's models.
January 2026: Grok generated sexualized images of children on X, prompting California investigation. Floor-relevant prior conduct sustained.
Take It Down Act FTC enforcement threshold active May 2026 (carry-over from May 3); no compliance certification disclosed.
35-state AG coalition + EU formal proceedings + 8+ countries acting (sustained evidence base).
